goalsSummary: One or two sentences: what the site is for (client language).
assetsSummary:
Bullet-style lines Hub puts in “What client already has”.
Example:
• Logo: on site (from client Instagram / brand files)
• Product photos: client-supplied, used in gallery
• Preview: (fill previewUrl)

confirmations.text: Logo — we used your brand mark from [source] on the site. Is this your confirmed logo?
Photos — product/gallery images on the site came from your materials. Keep these as the official set?
Preview site — the current build at [previewUrl] is for your business. Does it look like the right site?
Pages / sections — the site is set up as [e.g. single-page with Home, About, Shop, Contact]. Still correct?
Shopping — purchases go through [Etsy / other], not an on-site cart. Is that how you want it?
Contact form — people can reach you through the site form. Should messages go to [email]?
Social links — Instagram / Facebook (etc.) on the site point to your accounts. Are those the correct profiles?
Public launch — the site stays on a private/demo preview until you approve domain + go-live. Still correct?

notesForOperator: Edit confirmations after each meaningful build slice. Hub prefers this file over guessed inventory. Never put stack/Vite/React here.
